description: Unique identifier of a Service Request (SR) in the open data set. Each 311 service request is assigned a number that distinguishes it as a separate case incident.
The date and time that a Customer submits a Service Request.
The date and time that an Agency closes a Service Request.
Acronym of responding City Government Agency or entity responding to 311 Service Request.
Full agency name of responding City Government Agency, or entity responding to 311 service request.

This is the first level of a hierarchy identifying the topic of the incident or condition.  Problem (formerly Complaint Type) broadly describes the topic of the incident or condition and are defined by the responding agencies. New Problems may be added in response to changes in customer demand.

A second level of detail about the Problem (formerly Complaint Type), and provides further detail on the incident or condition. Problem Detail (formerly Descriptor) values are dependent on the Problem, and are not always required in the service request.
A third level of detail about the Problem (formerly Complaint Type) beyond the Problem Detail (formerly Descriptor). This is not used by every category of issue.
Describes the type of location used in the address information
Zip code of the incident address
House number and street name of incident address
Street name of incident address
First Cross street based on the geo validated incident location.
Second Cross Street based on the geo validated incident location
First intersecting street based on geo validated incident location
Second intersecting street based on geo validated incident location
Type of information available about the incident location.
City of the incident location. In this dataset, City can refer to a borough or neighborhood.
If the incident location is identified as a Landmark the name of the landmark will display here
If applicable, this field describes the type of city facility associated to the service request

Community boards are local representative bodies. There are 59 community boards throughout the City, each representing a distinct geography. For more information on Community Boards: http://www.nyc.gov/html/cau/html/cb/about.shtml
The City Council district where the service request is located.
The NYPD precinct where the service request is located

Parcel number that identifies the location of the building or property associated with the service request. The block is a subset of a borough. The lot is a subset of a block unique within a borough and block.
One of New York City's five boroughs

Geo validated, X coordinate of the incident location.

X coordinate of the incident location. For more information about NY State Plane Coordinate Zones: http://gis.ny.gov/gisdata/metadata/nysogs.statepln.html?nysgis=

Geo validated,  Y coordinate of the incident location.

Y coordinate of the incident location. For more information about NY State Plane Coordinate Zones: http://gis.ny.gov/gisdata/metadata/nysogs.statepln.html?nysgis=
Indicates how the service request was submitted to 311.
Geo based Latitude of the incident location in decimal degrees.
Geo based Longitude of the incident location in decimal degrees.
The combined geo-based latitude & longitude of the incident location.
If the incident location is a Parks Dept facility and service requests pertains to a facility managed by NYC Parks (DPR), the name of the facility will appear here
The borough of incident if the service request is pertaining to a NYC Parks Dept facility (DPR)
Data provided if service request pertains to a vehicle managed by the Taxi and Limousine Commision (TLC).
Data provided if service request pertains to a vehicle managed by the Taxi and Limousine Commision (TLC).
If the incident pertains a vehicle managed by the Taxi and Limousine Commision (TLC), this field displays the taxi pick up location
If the incident is identified as a Bridge/Highway, the name will be displayed here.
If the incident is identified as a Bridge/Highway, the direction where the issue took place would be displayed here.
If the incident location was Bridge/Highway this column differentiates if the issue was on the Road or the Ramp.
Additional information on the section of the Bridge/Highway were the incident took place.
